TextENGNew: "...Yes, that's right.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1002. The shipment has arrived as originally specified."
As the bearded man answered the caller on the other end of the line, he jerked his chin to signal the young man standing beside him.
The man nodded in response, then ran off to pick up one of the cardboard boxes piled up in another corner of the warehouse, then sliced a knife through the tightly wrapped packing tape.
The tape was cleanly cut and peeled off, then he pried open the lid.
Inside were a large number of repulsively colorful capsules wrapped in silver packaging. He pulled one out and held it up to the bearded man.

"I'll have the number of capsules checked, but it's only customary, so there should be no problems.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1004. After this, we'll proceed as planned and distribute them to the facilities designated by Chiba-sensei."
"Needless to say, avoid distributing to Gogura-affiliated hospitals and clinics for the time being."
"And thoroughly silence any doctors involved in the transaction.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_nomura1003. Since one of their own was just arrested, there's a chance they'll turn spineless and go to the police."
"No need to worry about that.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1006. I raised their kickback pay, so that'll be more than enough to convince them."
"Even Nishida, the doctor arrested by NCO, was apparently drowning in debt... so he was happy to comply when we offered to pay everything off after his release."
"That's good, but... for now, just be careful."
"Hahaha, you're always so anxious, Secretary."
The bearded man let out a bold laugh as he said that.
Even so... he was quite surprised when hundreds of millions were transferred from campaign funds in a single day to pay people for their silence.

From what he'd heard before, this woman he knew only by the title Secretary was acting as Chiba's treasurer... but this was a politician who had served as a Minister, so the amount of money behind him truly was vast.
"So... what's the situation...@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_nomura1006. regarding the new reagent?"

"Right...@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1010. It's written here that the new version of Placil should eliminate the problem of the subject regressing into an intoxicated state, as seen in previous clinical trials.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1011. ...Well, we won't know for sure until we try."

"But even in the previous version, the data was showing some degree of stability.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1013. Hopefully in the future this will become an antipsychotic medication as effective as C130 developed at the Irie Institute."

"...You'll just say anything, won't you?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_nomura1008. Still, Sensei must be cranky at how the Irie Institute's research results have such a great head start over his own international pharmaceutical company, no?"
"Hahaha, that's harsh.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1015. In return, I'll make an investment in the next offering."
The man laughed as he said that, then hung up the phone.
...Then he let out an exhausted sigh and spat on the floor to vent his displeasure.
"...Tending to Chiba-sensei must be pretty tough, Yamashiro-san."
"Hah... even this is nothing."

"It may only be in a medical institute in the Tokai area at best for now... but once this becomes an officially approved drug, hospitals all across the country will be begging for it.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1018. And my company will hold a monopoly as the wholesaler."
"I have no qualms bowing down to some politician if it means getting my hands on that golden egg."
"Well... the patients being used as guinea pigs don't have much to look forward to."
A man in sunglasses... Shirou Hanada shrugged his shoulders and laughed while handing an attache case to the bearded man called Yamashiro.
In fact, the patients taking this medication were content and completely oblivious to the fact that they were being prescribed a dangerous drug.
They trust what the doctors are telling them and continue taking the medicine so that doctors can study the efficacy and side effects, without knowing anything of the danger.

As a result, there were cases where the drug didn't sit well with the patient and actually made their condition worse... but that information was strictly sealed away, buried in the dark, never to be revealed.

"Even now, my company is still a cash-and-carry wholesaler dealing in illegal goods... but I want it to move beyond that and become a leader in the import and export of foreign pharmaceuticals.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1021. And I'm prepared to do a little tightrope walking to make that happen."
"How wonderful.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1125. And I'm sure Chiba-sensei will be there to support you in your endeavors."
As Hanada said that... deep down, he smirked maliciously.
If the new version of Placil is finalized... Chiba would probably set up another pharmaceutical company to be the contact point for Lowell Inc.
It wasn't clear yet if Yamashiro's company would be absorbed into some other organization or completely cut off... but either way, the probably of those dreams coming to fruition was close to zero.
(How sad for this trifling shopkeeper.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1127. His ambitions went soaring after a short meeting with a big-name politician.)

Still, Yamashiro had a reputation for being able to procure drugs cheaper than anyone else by buying up surplus inventory through backdoor sales, and he also had connections to many medical institutions.
So, he was the most suitable person to sell these illegal drugs through.
"More importantly... what are you going to do after this?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1023. I hear you're boarding the return flight and planning to leave the country...?"
"Well, I figured it would be best to lay low and rest until the situation cools off.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1129. With this much money, I should be more than able to indulge myself in a foreign land."
Hanada pointed to the duffel bag laying by his feet as he said that.

It was packed full of dollar bills. Banks and other financial institutions could freeze his assets, so he thought it was most prudent to request his payment in the most widely accepted form of foreign cash.

"If the ruling party wins the next election, it'll be Okuno-sensei as prime minister and Chiba-sensei right behind him.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1131. If that happens, the authorities won't dare touch me.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1132. No... more accurately, they couldn't touch me even if they wanted to."

"Hahaha.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1025. ...Aren't you worried that Chiba-sensei might eliminate you to keep you silent?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1026. If I were in his position, I wouldn't want to leave someone out there with as much dangerous information as you have."

"Ahahaha.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1134. I've already set up a certain route where secret documents will be disclosed to the media if he ever betrays me.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1135. He's welcome to try if he's willing to deal with that."
Yamashiro suppressed his discomfort that his threatening remark was brushed aside so lightly.
...Honestly, this man had a personality he could never get along with. And he was sure Hanada didn't like him either.
"But who could imagine a cop working as a spy for a politician?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1028. You know what they say. The world is at its end."

"In the end, police officers are like dogs.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1137. They'll wag their tail to the master of their family, but when it comes time to fight someone stronger than themselves, they're all bark and no bite."
"There are no principles behind it.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1139. Only the rule of power.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1140. So, wouldn't it be wisest to serve the stronger master?"

"Well... to each their own set of values, I suppose.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1030. Anyway, you've still got some time before the transport plane departs.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1031. The room in the back is empty, so now's a good time to get some rest."
"Yeah, I think I will.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1142. I've actually been staying at a cheap hotel in the area... and I wouldn't exactly call it comfortable."
Then, Hanada turned around and left. After watching him go, Yamashiro walked over to the shipment to count the packets...
Yes. In the end, justice is an illusion. Anyone who believes in it is an idiot.
I've seen it more times than I care to count.
Detectives aren't worth a damn. I... have no intention of turning back.
And this time... I swear I'll...!!
On Tomoe's order... the police vehicles split off into two separate groups, as their sirens blared out.
Then, car 11 with Fujita and Tomoe, as well as car 10 driven by young Makita, followed the highway toward the west side of the airport.

"...Cars 8 and 9, keep heading down the main street, then head east on prefectural route 56!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3084. Cars 12 and 13, turn right at the next intersection and go straight down Sugaya Town road, toward the south side of the airport!"
"This is car 8, roger!!"
"This is car 12, got it!"
...There were multiple routes in and out of Kakiuchi airport, so coming in from a single direction risked leaving another exit open.
Thus, approaching from multiple directions would convince the enemy that they're surrounded and prevent them from trying to escape.
(Although, a handful of police cars really isn't enough to hold a blockade...)
So if this turns into a long-term battle, there was a good chance the enemy would realize they had an advantage in numbers, which might allow them to turn the situation around.
So they'd just have to hold down the scene and capture the key suspects before then. ...That was the primary objective.
And...
"Asami-san, how many vehicles do you have?"

"Right!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_asami1040. At the moment, cars 2 and 7 are on Shimojibashi Way, heading to the southern end of Kakiuchi airport, and cars 4 and 5 are passing by city hall, in East Oumaki!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_asami1041. I'm in car 3... Oh, I've got a visual on your car now!"
"I see you too!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3087_1. Asami-san, get in formation next to my car, car 11!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3088. We'll start our advance from the west side of the airport!"
"Roger!!"
"All cars, turn off your sirens!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3090. First Division, head to the north side of the airport and secure the entrance to the SDF base!"
"Second Division, enter the terminal building and search for Shirou Hanada! And see to the safety of any bystanders in case this turns into a gunfight!"
""Got it!!""
"Second Division, follow Asami-san's command!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3093. Be careful! Securing the safety of the general public is your top priority!"
"Leave it to us!"
Tomoe delivered orders one after another over the police radio. Watching her through a sideways glance, Fujita let out a sigh of admiration.

"M-Minai-san...!@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1226. How are you able to give such detailed orders without even looking at a map?@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1227. Even as a local of this area, there's no way I'd be able to do that...!"
"...Because I've been pounding away at this...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3095. for twelve years straight."
Tomoe grinned and shrugged her shoulders.
...She had personally walked around this airport in her spare time and carefully studied the map many times since her father's death.
Always believing there was some clue hidden in the message her father left behind.
So, when it came to which routes to take to reach the airport and the fastest way to travel around them... Tomoe more than had it covered.
Plus, whether the criminals stayed on the airport grounds, or headed to the adjacent SDF base... Tomoe had already imagined how any such scenario would play out during her free time.
Although, she never actually expected those thought exercises to come in handy someday...

"However, none of the investigators here today are carrying firearms.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3097. Meanwhile, most of the enemies are likely armed...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3098. Even if numbers are on our side, we're still at a disadvantage."
"Yeah... definitely."
Since they can't use firearms, they had no choice but to directly approach the enemies and capture them. And doing so put the investigators at considerable risk.
So they should have a suicide corp sneak in once they arrive and prioritize holding down the scene.
Then, maybe they could manage to arrest some of them as they tried to flee through a hole in the escape route...
"Fujita-kun,@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3100. did you pack bulletproof vests?"
"Of course. They're in the trunk.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1230. And we prepared everything for everyone else, too."
"...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3102.Sounds like you were planning to come even if I said no."

"Well, obviously.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1232. I'd rather deal with the crying and abuse from Yuko instead of being beaten by everyone else for abandoning my boss.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1233. And please stop dwelling on these things this far into the operation."
"...Thanks."
Needless to say, the risk of heading inside was far greater than just injury... it could mean death.
But to hear Fujita say he was willing to follow her into that... even without any sort of romantic sentiment behind it, it truly made her happy.
"...This is Hayami of the Mobile Investigation Team at prefectural police HQ!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1002. Princess, please respond!"
Just then, a familiar voice came in over the radio.
There was no mistaking it... that was Captain Hayami, leader of the first Mobile Investigation Team.
"Hayami-san...?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3106. Wh-what's going on?"
"Don't you "what's going on" at me!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1004. How dare you not send us an invitation for this delightful party?!"
"Yeah, that's right!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1002. We're professionals specifically trained as riot police, yet you'd leave us sitting on our asses?!"
"Yoshikawa-san?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3108. Even the second Mobile Investigation Team is getting involved...? Why?!"
Tomoe quickly regained her composure, then grabbed the receiver and spoke in her usual tone.

Hayami and Yoshikawa were the leaders of the two respective Mobile Investigation Teams at prefectural HQ, responsible for preliminary investigations and also for organizing raids against armed criminal groups.
In a sense, they were a specialized unit of gunmen intended for investigating organized crime... So why were they here?

"We heard the story from Doujima-san!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1004. Don't tell me tickets were sold out the day they went on sale.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1005. Please, at least give us a little piece of the action!"
"You can just step aside and watch if you'd like.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1006. We're gonna barge right in and have a great time!"
"W-wait a minute...!"
It was very encouraging... but more than that, it was so unexpected that Tomoe was confused and at a loss for words.
Hayami said he heard it from Doujima. ...Since he's in charge of joint investigation HQ, he may have heard something from Maeno.
And he probably figured we'd be up against a large number of armed foes. So he called in Hayami and Yoshikawa in anticipation of that...? No way?!
"Hayami-san, Yoshikawa-san, please turn back!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3111. Even if you come all the way out here... there's nothing you can do, you know?!"
...Right.
Tomoe made a point to resign from the police force and head into this investigation alone because she wanted to avoid causing trouble for all her colleagues who supported her.
And yet... now the First Division, the Second Division, and even MIT at prefectural HQ were all joining together to fight for her sake.
...She was delighted.
But the personnel from prefectural HQ can't afford to act on their own discretion like this.
One misstep and this could lead to total war between the police and the Defense Agency...!

"Please, both of you!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3113. I'm glad that you feel that way... Really, I'm very grateful!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3114. But if prefectural HQ gets involved, it'll be more than just my neck on the line!"
"Haha, excellent!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1008. I'd love to hear someone try to say that arresting a criminal is a crime worth getting arrested over!"
"Seriously, treason?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1007. Hah!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1008. It's been ages since Japan had a crime like this!"

"Princess!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1010. Don't say another word!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1011. If you do, we'll just tie you down and keep heading in without you, all right?!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1012. It would be pathetic for prefectural investigators not to show up on a scene with armed criminals!!"
...Hayami's words were crude... but right now, they felt very warm.
That man had helped her many times, and scolded her any time she made a mistake.
And, now and in the future...!
"Kakiuchi First and Second Division, listen up!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1014. Yoshikawa's team will help protect the civilians at the airport. Got that, Asami?!"
"R... right!!"
"All right!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1016. Work with Yoshikawa for operational command!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1017. And First Division, stay behind us!"
"Don't engage any armed perps! You don't stand a chance against them!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1010. We're going to hit the building with tear gas, so you guys can seize anyone running out!!"
"R-roger!"
"U... understood!!"
"Hayami-san... Yoshikawa-san...!!"

"Come on, no need to worry. Doujima-san will take care of it!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1019. We received reports that a person nearby heard gunshots, so we rushed to the scene, all right?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1020. With that justification, it all makes sense, doesn't it?"

"Well, the timing may be a little out of order... but surely that's just a clerical error, yeah.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1012. We just happened to be on patrol nearby, so we were able to get to the scene very quickly."
"What kind of excuse is that?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1022. Ha ha ha!!"
Hayami and Yoshikawa's boisterous laughter came pouring in through the radio.
It was strange. Listening to all these people, the sense of despair she'd been feeling up until this point was gradually transforming into something genuinely cheerful...
And then...
"Come in! This is the Public Security first division from prefectural HQ!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1079. You hot-rodders rushing to the scene, do you read me?"
"A-Akiyama-san?!"
Tomoe suddenly jumped as another voice came in over the radio. It was Akiyama, the director of the Public Security division at prefectural police.
"Sheesh... you guys can act on your own accord for all I care, but raiding an SDF facility without a warrant?!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1081. Well don't worry, because I got you one!"
"What's that, Akiyama?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1014. Are you gonna come rushing in with a bazooka or a tank?"
"Screw you, dumbass!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1083. It's a search warrant!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1084. I had to wake up a district court judge in the middle of the night to get this, so be grateful!"
"It... it can't be?!"
It was such an unexpected gift that Tomoe couldn't help but blurt that out.
Based on her past experience dealing with government agencies... getting a warrant to enter a Defense Agency facility was by far the most time consuming to obtain.
And yet... they got one issued on the same day?! That's absolutely, completely impossible!!
"A...Akiyama-san...!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3121. How the hell did you do that...?!"
"Yeah, that's incredible!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1024. What sort of magic did you use?!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1025. The courts hardly ever allow searches of SDF facilities!!"

"...Hah!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1086. It's all thanks to you, Princess.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1087. That information you obtained on the smuggling operation made the section chief of Public Security livid in a way nobody's seen in ages!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1088. So he wanted to strike back immediately!"
Leaking or smuggling information to countries is contrary to national interest, so strictly speaking, it was the sort of thing Public Security had to nip in the bud as soon as possible.

And the fact that it involved a precinct detective meant that Public Security detectives had their dignity on the line. So, Akiyama and his colleagues were dispatched in order to assist in the search...!
"I-I'm sorry... Akiyama-san.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3123. It's all because of me..."

"...Yeah, that's right! It's all because of you!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1090. So next time, you better bring Public Security some more of that local sake, all right?!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1091. That stuff is so good!!"
"Yeah, and bring some for MIT too!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1016. A dozen bottles should be enough, all right, Princess?"
""Ahahahahah!!!""
Despite all the lively laughter... Tomoe was biting her lip and fighting to suppress the heat forming in the corner of her eyes.
All of this.
These veteran detectives were doing so much to support her even after she tried to run off and act alone.
But she was not alone.
And justice was not a selfish act...!
"Then as soon as everyone's in position, let's press the attack!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1028. Everyone, are you ready and prepared?!"
""Yeaaaaaahhh!!""
"Princess!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1030. We're not as familiar with the airport layout as you are, so you should handle positioning orders!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1031. Are you up for it?!"
...Before long.
The police vehicles had all stopped at their respective positions according to Tomoe's orders, with their sirens turned off.
Tomoe's group was parked in front of the central gate. Hayami quietly approached the staff member at the gate and held out his police badge, then the iron gate gently slid open.
"...This is Team A. All ready to rush in."
"...This is Team B. Ready when you are."
"...This is the terminal rush group. All investigators are in position."
After Asami's report, the radio went quiet... and the surrounding area was silent.
And then...
"...All cars!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3127. Move in!!!"
On that shout, all the sirens began roaring all at once, and their flashing lights dyed the entire area in bright red.
Immediately after that, the riot vehicles under Hayami's command began rushing the base all at once...!
"Th-this is bad!!"
"...What's going on?"
"I-it's the police!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_manA1003. They're here, and they're coming in!!"
"Wh-what?!"
Just as Yamashiro was surprised by a guardsman rushing in with urgent news, that same moment a sound roared out like a bomb exploding.
Immediately after that, several windows were blown to pieces... and the room was filled with white smoke.
"Th-this is...?!"
"I-it's tear gas!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_manA1005. ...Guh?!"
The pungent odor sent severe pain running through his nose, and tears began overflowing from his eyes nonstop.
Everyone working inside had that same exact reaction, and many of them couldn't resist the urge to get out, so they rushed to the door and opened it.
"...?!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1035. You idiots, don't go outside!!"
Yamashiro shouted at them to stop, but countless shots rang out faster than they could respond, and some people fell to the ground after getting hit in their limbs.
"Gwah?!"
"Gyaah?!"
The people who had been shot were writhing about in agony. But apparently, nobody among them was mortally wounded.
These were likely rubber bullets used in riot control. As a former Self Defense officer, Yamashiro was the only one who realized that meant that the riot police were here.

"Ahem, attention all criminals inside the warehouse.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1033. I repeat, attention all criminals inside the warehouse.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1034. Stop this futile resistance and come out quietly.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1035. If you do, I can grant you the right to a lawyer and the right to remain silent, on the house~."
"Aren't those... normal rights that everyone is guaranteed anyway?"
"Huh, is that so?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1037. I always thought that lawbreakers were only given a single choice between Heaven and Hell."
"And what law is that written in...?"
""Aahahaha!!""
Yamashiro and his henchmen could hear this comedic exchange through the loudspeaker.
One man was so beside himself with rage that he began screaming and started to dash outside... but within a few steps he was hit by sniper fire and fell to the ground.
"Shoot! Return fire!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1037. They're not real bullets! They're not deadly!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1038. Push them back and escape!"
Yamashiro screamed hard, trying to inspire his men.
But... these weren't battle-hardened yakuza with experience in gunfights. They were just small-time thugs.
They had no chance in a fair fight... It was impossible!
"Damn it... Why?!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1040. Why are these cops pulling guns on us?!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1041. Don't they know the law?!"
Another round of shots roared out, as if to answer his abusive words.
An outlaw cursing the police for acting outside the law. ...Yamashiro himself probably didn't realize the humorous contradiction there.
"I-it's over...!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_manD1002. We're gonna get arrested...?!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_manD1003. U-uwaaaaaahh!!"
"Shove it! If you've got time to cry, grab a gun!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1043. Fire! Fire, fiiiire!!"
"...Whoa?!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_kisouA1002. They're returning fire!"
"Hmph! ...Amateurs, the lot of them.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1040. They'll never hit anything firing into the darkness from a well-lit area."

"Anyway, this meets the legal requirement for us to take self-defense measures!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1042. The suppression level has now been promoted to level AA!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1043. If you see an opportunity, feel free to snipe the personnel in the building too!"
"Roger that!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_kisouA1004. ...But Hayami-san, you're acting like a scoundrel here too."
"You're firing blanks and launching projectiles with a catapult at the same time to threaten them.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_kisouA1006. I'm sure those guys are really shaken in there!"
"It's not a catapult! Call it a slingshot!"
It was a device with a rubber band stretched across a Y shaped rod, just like kids played with to launch stones.
But this device had specialized rubber and was shaped to maximize range, so it would have enough power to take down human foes.

It was originally sold in another country as a hunting weapon, but Hayami noticed it during overseas training and bought up a large quantity of them to be deployed to his men as an alternative to guns.
"But... it's really fortunate that you found a weapon like this.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_kisouB1002. People made fun of it and treated it like a toy at first, but it actually packs an impressive punch."
"Yeah... I'm glad you guys were patient and stuck with me.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1046. Thanks to that, we were able to actually use it in battle...!"
Even as they chatted, one of the armed criminals in the warehouse moaned and collapsed on the spot after receiving a lead projectile to the face.
The enemies immediately began focusing their gunfire toward the source of the gunshot sound, but the officers anticipated that and slipped behind the duralumin shield to easily escape harm.
The firing point and the source of the gunfire sound weren't even the same in the first place. And in this darkness, there was hardly any risk of a sniper actually hitting them...!
"...Use of firearms is severely restricted for us at the Japanese police.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1048. Anyone firing a gun can expect to be chewed out by the top brass."
"That's the harsh reality. It's hard to stand up to armed thugs without them, but we're still expected to fight them."
"So, I just took matters into my own hands searching for a suitable alternative.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1051. ...Is that so wrong?"
"No, not at all."
(...Plus, this way we avoid planting any seeds that could open the Princess up to criticism.)
Hayami was very calculating with his concerns, understanding that even MIT was prohibited from using live ammunition against armed criminals.
This bluff would probably come undone in a prolonged battle... but let's cross that bridge when the time comes!
"Anyway, don't do anything rash!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1054. And pass that message along to the guys in the First Division, too!!"
"Understood!"
After that brisk reply, his subordinates quickly bowed and then left. After seeing them off, Hayami gently gazed at the dials of his glow in the dark wristwatch.
"I'm counting on you... Doujima-san, Yamamura-san.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1056. And..."
"Ch-Chief!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hata1002. Is the Chief here?!"
When Deputy Chief Hata headed into the Chief's office with Okabe, he found Yamamura and Doujima already in the room, sitting across from each other and playing a game of shogi.
"...Check."
"Hmm? Ahh...@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamamu1006. Now, how should I respond to that?"
"Wh... what are you people doing?!"
"What are we doing...? Can't you tell just looking?"
He glared at Yamamura with his fangs exposed and a look clearly saying "that's not what I'm asking."
What a stubborn old man. Plus, he couldn't be cajoled because he wasn't one to be swayed by money or emotions, and he was far too popular to effectively get rid of him.
"...More importantly, the Chief!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hata1005. Where is the Chief?!"
"Oh, if you're looking for the Chief, he already went home.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamamu1009. His wedding is coming up really soon, so I'm sure he's got his hands full with the preparations."
"H-huuuh?!"

"Man, I'm so jealous.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1075. He was always so stiff and not much of a ladies man, but he managed to win over such a young girl.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1076. Who knew he was so young at heart?"
"Totally.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamamu1011. I'd throw away my career if it could get me a wife that young.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamamu1012. ...Oh, here's my next move."
"Hoh? In that case, I'll..."
"Enough with the bullshit!!"
Unable to endure it any longer, Hata screamed and slammed the handkerchief he was holding down on the shogi board.
Countless pieces scattered across the floor, but Yamamura and Doujima didn't look particularly surprised. In fact, they calmly turned to face Hata, whose was looking rather flustered.

"What the hell is going on?!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hata1009. There are apparently officers engaged in a gunfight at Kakiuchi airport at this very moment!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hata1010. Are you guys trying to start a war?!"

"Oh, don't worry.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1079. I've got my men, Hayami and Yoshikawa, supporting them, so there's no way they'll lose.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1080. Maybe I should call in the members of the investigation division too?"
"Don't be stupid!!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hata1012. A shootout inside an SDF base?! Are you guys crazy?!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hata1013. Just how many enemies are you trying to make!?!"

"The Ministry of Transport, the Defense Agency, and the Ministry of Foreign Affairs... We're shooting for the triple jackpot.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1082. Wait, there's also the Ministry of Health and Welfare... so does that make it quadruple?"
"If you're aware of it, then why aren't you stopping this?!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hata1015. Don't you see the liability issue?!"
As Hata continued shouting more and more, Yamamura and Doujima looked at one another for a moment, then sighed and turned away from Hata to pick up the scattered shogi pieces.
It was abundantly clear why HR back at headquarters decided against promoting Hata to Chief of Kakiuchi station when the opportunity came up.

This man was focused first and foremost on preserving his own position. If he was in a position of responsibility and first heard about this uproar, finding out what the investigation was all about should've been his top priority.
(I felt a little sorry for him, but it seems even that was a mistake.)
With that in mind, Doujima gave Yamamura a signal. Probably picking up on his intentions, Yamamura shook his head and shrugged his shoulders.

"A...anyway!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hata1017. Who do you think is going to take responsibility for the misconduct of your subordinates?!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hata1018. This isn't something that can be downplayed by firing a few people, you know?!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hata1019. It'll go all the way to the Chief himself..."
"Oh, if you're asking about who's going to be held responsible,@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamamu1015. then take a good look at this."
Hata's eyes opened wide as soon as he saw what Yamamura was pointing at.
It was two official notices of resignation. From Yamamura... and Yamaoki.
"And I just mailed one of my own to the chief of prefectural HQ.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1086. ...Anyway, let's start another match."
"Y...you guys... Why?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hata1022. Why are you willing to go so far to stick up for them?!"

"Why?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamamu1017. ...Well, if you feel the need to ask that in a situation like this, I'm not sure you'd understand it no matter what I said.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamamu1018. Yeah... there's no way you could possibly understand."
Upon witnessing that quiet but dignified strength up close, Hata couldn't help but swallow his spit.
...The weaker the dog, the louder their bark.

Hata was certainly older than Doujima, but the man who greeted him as the section chief of an investigation division at prefectural police when Hata was first promoted to Deputy Chief was of an entirely different order than him. ...No, they were completely incomparable.
Hata always expected people to listen to him because of his title and seniority, so the two of them could never see eye to eye.
"Th... this is nonsense!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hata1025. Okabe-kun, call in the staff from the Second Division! Stop them!"
"What's wrong?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hata1027. Hurry up alrea... huh?!"
Without any hesitation.
Okabe had been silent since they entered the room, but he crossed in front of Hata... then took a letter out of his pocket and gently placed it beside the other two letters of resignation.
And right on the cover of the page was the word "resignation" written in meticulous calligraphy.
"O...Okabe?!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hata1029. What?! You too?!"

"...@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1055.When the girl first came here, I hated her.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1056. Her bottomless initiative, her incredibly quick thinking... and her surprisingly stubborn perseverance.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1057. Whenever I saw it, I took it as a threat and felt unpleasant... even envious."
"So, I was sure she'd end up my superior in just a few short years... and when I thought about that I couldn't help but feel pathetic in comparison."
And that... was why Okabe always harbored a sense of animosity towards Tomoe. He was upset to learn of her success and pleased to hear of her failures.
...But he had finally noticed the ungraceful pettiness of his heart.
Looking back, though it was far too late, he finally realized his own mistake...
"Honestly... I was sick of this.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1060. I questioned why I was acting like such a terrible person."
"I didn't become a police officer just to be a section chief at some local precinct..."
"Wh-what are you saying...?!"
Yes, it wasn't that long ago...
Okabe suddenly remembered... the conversation he accidentally overheard in front of the police station, when Tomoe addressed the detectives of the First Division.
He once had a detective's spirit like that, too.
There was certainly a time he felt passionate about catching the bad guys and helping the good guys.
And, he noticed that Asami and the other folks in the Second Division had really come alive recently, and regained their hearts.

"Plus with this, that girl will owe me a favor.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1063. No matter how high she climbs in the ranks... I'll be able to hold my head up to her because of what I did today.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1064. And that's enough to keep me satisfied."
As he said that.
Okabe's face had a thoroughly furrowed brow up until a moment ago... but now he had a refreshing smile on his face, as though freed from whatever possessed him.
For such a long time... he'd been feeling truly sullen and unsatisfied.
Bowing down to a boss he hated just to protect his current position... sometimes even reluctantly engaging in behavior he knew was wrong.

And at the same time, he felt both envy and animosity toward this young elite who was always energetic and believed in justice from the bottom of her heart... but he felt disgusted with himself for having such a servile attitude.

"But... this is enough.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1066. With this, the weight on my shoulders is just a little lighter.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1067. All that's left is for the youngsters to try as hard as they can."
"Th... this is nonsense!!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hata1032. Now that it's come to this, I'll head straight to the main office to tell them about this lunacy, and...@k@|@y@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hata1033. Wha?!"
Before he even realized it... Yamamura and Doujima had moved the sofa in front of the door and sat down on it, facing Hata.
They definitely had smiles on their faces... but there was a strange glint in their eyes as they stared straight at him.

"Come on, Deputy Chief.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1088. We went to all this trouble, so let's hang out a while longer.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1089. ...It's just a few more days anyway.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1090. You've had your share of good experiences, so isn't that enough to be satisfied?"
"Y-you son of a bitch...!!"
"For police officers, sometimes it's good to be a little stupid.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamamu1020. ...Just look at those youngsters."
"Attention criminals, you really ought to surrender already.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1058. Otherwise we'll be forced to deploy a wrecking ball on the building, all right~?"
"Whoa... you're seriously going all out!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_kisouA1008. Are you trying to destroy the entire warehouse?!"

"No, no... cleanup afterwards would be such a hassle, so let's just call in a fire truck and blast them with water!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1020. I hear it's gonna be cold tonight, so maybe that'll help our heated foes cool off?"
"Ah, in that case let's use the chemical fire response vehicle!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_kisouB1006. If we splash them with soapy water, their filthy faces will come out squeaky clean!"
""Wa ha ha ha ha!!""
"D... damn it...!!"
Yamashiro hurled expletives as he gazed out the window, while rubbing his eyes and his nose which were now bright red from the tear gas.
Detectives had already entered the premises, so they could be clearly seen.

Meanwhile, they had limited weapons at their disposal to return fire, and they were running low on ammunition. Even if they rushed out all at once, they'd be able to take a few of them down but there'd be no chance of escape.
"Why...?!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1046. Why did it end up like this...?!"
He clung to his head and clenched his teeth so tight they were about to shatter.
...It wasn't supposed to be like this.
That secretary told him there was nothing to worry about because this deal was backed by a senator. And a raid should've been impossible because the deal was happening on SDF grounds.
That's why a man as risk-averse as Yamashiro was willing to take this job.
(...And yet, look at this mess!)
Yamashiro continued cursing out his employer, while frantically looking around for an escape route.

At any rate, now was the time to either run or hide. As long as he could do that, Chiba and that secretary could use their powers to secure a hiding place for him, and maybe help him escape to a foreign country like they did for Hanada.
But... where could he run to?
There were detectives ready and waiting at every exit. Even if he left through a window, it would end all the same.
And even if he could get up to the roof, that was no escape route because he'd have to come back down to the ground eventually.
In which case... all that was left was...
"...That's it!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yamashi1049. Hey, have the remaining guys check the basement and search for a manhole or something!"
Upon hearing that order, the grunts who were feeling desperate and defeated suddenly gained a little light in their eyes.
He heard that Kakiuchi airport has an elaborate drainage system, since the facility performs double duty as an SDF base.
By going through the sewers, they might be able to escape the facility without being noticed by the surrounding investigators...!
"...Aha!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_manC1003. Boss, we found it!"
"You idiot!"
When the young man's bouncy voice echoed from the corner of the room, Yamashiro rushed over there and knocked him in the face to silence him.
If the people outside had heard that, it would all be for nothing.
"Is this it...?"
After removing the manhole, he could see an escape route open up beneath the surface.
He once read an article that said the Kakiuchi airport sewer system spread out over a wide, complicated area.
"If I go through here...!"
Yamashiro rubbed his nose while ordering some of the remaining men to remove the iron grate blocking the exit and descend the old ladder.
And then, everyone stepped into the footpath inside the sewer system, but just then...
"U-uwaaaaaaaahhh!!!"
"...This is group 3 reporting in. Suspects have been secured."
"Don't relax just yet. There may still be others who fled through a different route.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1061. ...Has there been any word from groups 1 and 2?"

"Group 1 reports that one member was injured in an altercation with an enemy fleeting through the back door, but the injuries are not life threatening.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_kisouC1003. They were able to secure three suspects taking shelter from the gas in the corner of the room."

"Group 2 rushed in from the front, but did not encounter any of the criminals.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_takaya1042. Instead, it seems they were able to seize a large number of cardboard boxes containing medicine capsules."
"I see..."
Hayami lit a cigarette and took a breath.
...They had to rush to take measures according to Tomoe's advice, but it seems they managed to get it done.

If they hadn't noticed it and taken precautions in advance, the criminals might've been able to not just escape out the back, but potentially do some unexpected damage too. ...He couldn't help but break out in a cold sweat just imagining it.
"...But I never even considered they might use an underground sewer system as an escape route.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1064. Has the Princess engaged in anti-terrorism warfare before?"
"Well, she said she recently heard a story about a house built with a secret escape route in the basement, and it made her think.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_kisouC1005. ...It's quite remarkable."
"If she ever gets fired from the Public Relations Office, maybe she could become a tactical advisor for us and even lecture our men on the subject?"

"You dumbass!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1066. That girl has her sights set far higher than that.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1067. I'd worry that just making her step foot in such a place would cause her talents to rot!"
"...Right.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_kisouC1008. If possible, it'd be best to bring them back in one piece."
Hearing his subordinate talking over the radio, Hayami responded "Yeah."

The police force is also part of the political landscape. You have to bend yourself a certain way in order to rise in the ranks, and avoid sticking your neck out in dangerous situations that could tarnish your reputation.
That's why elites often look to their superiors for support, and try to keep their subordinates under control so they don't cause problems.
But... sometimes things go wrong, and a fool with true ideals and beliefs will emerge.
Someone so serious about fighting crime that they'll even force work on their superiors to get it done. And someone capable of organizing analog information just from their knowledge alone.
...A person like that invokes expectations even beyond their ability. That's why we have to set aside our emotions to protect people like that.
By doing that, you can get a breath of fresh air and believe that the situation will improve...
That was why Hayami and his men were supporting Tomoe.
"...As prideful as Maeno is, he bowed his head and begged us to do it.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1068. So, we'll figure something out.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1069. Let's live up to those expectations."
"Of course.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_kisouC1010. Let's show them that even politicians should be right to fear the power of the police force."
"Absolutely."
After saying that, Hayami stomped out his cigarette and ordered his subordinates to head into the warehouse.
Then after seeing them off, he turned around and rushed to the scene where investigators were detaining the suspects arrested in the sewer.
"Haah, haah, haah...!!"
Drenched in mud and sweat, Hanada ran down the runway in the direction the transport aircraft should be parked.
The moment he heard that explosion, he knew it was tear gas. So, Hanada quickly rushed into the bathroom and jumped out the window to escape from the warehouse as soon as possible.
"Why...? Damn it, why?!"
Based on all the information he gathered and all the time he spent inside the police station, he was sure he had a handle on their habits and thought processes.
So, he was secretly relieved because he was certain that they'd never be able to overcome the friction necessary to enter an SDF base.
And yet... how?!
They were desperately rampaging through the base, engaging in a firefight like something out of a country beset by raging civil war.
It's like that parable about stepping on a sleeping cat's tail, only to learn it was actually a tiger. If things stay as they are, the situation will only get worse and worse.
...But, where could he run to?!
The investigators were going in boldly... so they almost certainly had staff inside the terminal building, too. Nothing good would come from heading there.
That being said, this was an airfield so the surrounding area had high visibility and not a lot of places to hide.

And, if he was arrested... it would be all over. Just the charges that were made public would be enough to land him a heavy sentence, but if things went particularly poorly he could even be facing the death penalty...!
"I can't let that happen...!!"
That moment... Hanada caught a glimpse of the warehouse where the transport plane was being refueled.
Fortunately, the plane was scheduled to depart soon, so the engine should still be warm.
If that's the case... then...!!
"I can do it...!@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1148. I'll run as far as I have to, and I'll survive this...!!"
The only way left to escape from this predicament was abandoning Japan itself...!
With that resolution in his heart, Hanada resumed rushing toward the plane. He had no time to waste being hesitant.

"This is Asami!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_asami1048. We are currently seeing to the safety of the passengers in the terminal while searching for Shirou Hanada, but at this time his whereabouts are still unknown!"

"This is Yoshikawa!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1023. We received intel that a person resembling Hanada was witnessed at a snack shop, but it seems he hasn't completed boarding procedures yet!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1024. We are expanding the search area to include the vicinity of the airport entrance, and will continue the investigation!"

Investigators scattered to search all four corners of the terminal building, without leaving anything unsearched or any opportunities to escape, and were exchanging detailed information over their radios.
But... they still hadn't located their primary target, Hanada.
The airport staff were sure he hadn't taken off yet, so the investigators were frantically searching to see if there was another way out.
"Yoshikawa-san!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_asami1050. Are you sure Shirou Hanada wasn't among the perps you captured?"
"I told you already!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1026. He's not here!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1027. Where the hell could he have disappeared to...?"
Yoshikawa clicked his tongue while quickly scanning his surroundings.
According to the men Hayami and his colleagues arrested in the warehouse, Hanada was at the warehouse just a little while ago talking to their boss, Yamashiro.
So at the very least, he was still on the grounds of this airport.
But... where the hell could he be...?!
"Asami!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1029. Is there a possibility that he might've noticed something was up and escaped from the terminal building?"

"I... can't say it's impossible!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_asami1052. But investigators from the Second Division and from the HQ Public Security division have been watching the building perimeter, so it should be extremely difficult to get out without being discovered!"
"I think we're better off investigating the possibility that he's still there!"
"...That's right!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1031. Hey, Princess!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1032. Where else could someone hide in this airport?!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1033. ...Princess?"
"Hello?! Where did you go at a time like this?! Please respond!!"
"...?!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1072. O-oh shit!!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1073. The runway!!"
When Hayami's sudden shout came in over the radio, Yoshikawa rushed out of the building and turned toward the runway.
And then...
"Wh...what?!"
He saw a plane emerge from a distant hangar and slowly turn toward the runway.
And as proof of what it was, the illuminated tail fin bore the characteristic logo of Central Airlines.
Based on the information they already had, it would be easy to infer this was the transport plane that carried in the cargo.

"I just heard it from Yamashiro, who we caught in the sewers!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1075. Shirou Hanada is on that plane!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1076. Apparently, he was planning to board that plane and escape overseas!"
*bang*
Yoshikawa slammed his fist into the chain link fence in front of him, filled with regret.
When they heard that Hanada was heading to Kakiuchi airport, they focused on searching the terminal building... but neglected that the airport also coexisted with the SDF base.
If only they'd realized that... Shit!!
"Asami!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1038. Quickly, contact the control tower. Tell them to stop that transport aircraft!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1039. We can't let it take off, no matter what!!"

"That's impossible!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_asami1055. We're too far away from the control tower!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_asami1056. Besides, the control tower operators don't have the authority to revoke take-off clearance from a military craft!"

"Even if it's crazy and impossible, do something!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1041. If that plane makes it up in the air, we're done for!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1042. If nothing we say can stop it, then hit it with a car or someth...@k@|@y@vS14/00/VT4Bb_yoshika1043. Wha?!"
Just then.
A single police car broke through the chain link fence just a short distance away from Yoshikawa and his men... barreling down the runway at tremendous speed.
...Who is that?
Who is driving that thing?
No way.
It can't be... Her...?!
There were cracks spread across the windshield like a spider web, probably damaged while breaking through that chain-link fence.
The roar of the engine echoed all throughout the car, and it was flying forward at incredible speed, with the accelerator pushed to the limit.
The surrounding scenery was flying backwards at blinding speed, and the G forces pushing her back against the seat were strong enough to violently shake her body, and even her heart.
And, right before Tomoe's eyes...
She could hear the sound of the jet engines... and she could clearly see the plane was just about to start accelerating for takeoff.
"...I won't let you go...!"
Tomoe crossed the runway, then turned the wheel to steer her car directly in front of the transport plane.
She figured chasing the plane from the side and trying to hit the landing gear would be too difficult an approach.
But... no, because of that, she decided that she shouldn't bother chasing the plane... and instead, should directly attack it from the front!!
"W...wait, Princess!!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_hayami1078. Come back!! This is crazy!!"
"P...please stop, Minai-san!!@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1236. Are you suicidal?!"
The sorrowful screams of Fujita and the others came bursting out of the radio so intensely that the speakers were about to pop off, and echoed throughout the car.
But... she couldn't stop. There's no way she would.
"You think I'll let you gooooo!?!"
The jet was now in takeoff posture and approaching at tremendous speed.
Even with the windows closed, her eardrums and the inside of her head were violently shaking from the sound like a roaring beast.
Nevertheless, her eyes remained fixated on a single point straight ahead... She had a clear view of the wheel under the left wing.
"I won't let you escape...!!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3133. You... You...!!"
He robbed her of her mother and father.... who she loved and deeply respected.
He scarred her sister with wounds that would last a lifetime... cursed her to many years of hatred and resentment.
He brutally destroyed her precious treasure, her happy world... No matter how many times she killed him, it wouldn't be enough.
(No... that's enough of that.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3135. More than that... More than that!!)
Right now, the unshaking resolve pushing her to step down on the accelerator was for something more important.
...Yes.
The man right before her, who was ridiculing her as he tried to fly away right now... was an enemy of Tomoe, her father, and the entire police force.
He bent the law to wield power for personal gain.
He abused a just cause and did something terrible... He was an affront to justice. He was evil incarnate.
He was absolute scum who took the lives of innocent people and even trampled on the blood and tears they shed!!
She couldn't forgive him.
Not now. Not ever.
Even if this attack is throwing straws against the wind... the justice the police spoke of must never retreat, and never give in!!
So!!
"You're not going anywhere!!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3137. ...I won't let you goooooo!!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe3138. Aaaaaaaaahhh!!!"
